task: I need to turn a messy internal note into a short update for a few people, and I want to make sure it’s clear enough that they can follow it quickly. Can you help me shape it into something I can send?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely. If you paste the note, I can turn it into a short update for you.

If you want, I can shape it in one of these styles:

- **plain internal update** — short and direct
- **more polished** — still simple, just cleaner
- **calm/neutral** — good if people are already frustrated
- **action-focused** — what’s happening, what’s next, when to expect another update

A simple format that usually works is:

> **What happened:**  
> [1 sentence]
>
> **What we’re doing:**  
> [1 sentence]
>
> **What they need to know:**  
> [1 sentence]
>
> **Next update:**  
> [time/date or “as soon as we have it”]

If you send the messy note, I’ll condense it without making it sound vague or overexplained.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. Here’s the note, rough as it is:

“Still waiting on final confirmation from ops. There was a scheduling issue tied to the vendor changeover, so a couple of the deliverables got pushed. Nothing is canceled, but the timing is slipping. I’m checking whether we can keep the original deadline for the main piece or if we need to adjust. Will update once I hear back from them.”

I need it short, clear, and not dramatic. Just something people can read fast and know what’s going on.

turns.agent_response:
Here’s a short version you can send:

> We’re still waiting on final confirmation from ops after a scheduling issue tied to the vendor changeover. A couple of deliverables have shifted, but nothing is canceled. I’m checking whether the main deadline can stay as planned or needs to move, and I’ll update you as soon as I hear back.

If you want, I can also make it:
- a little more casual
- more polished
- even shorter for Slack/Teams

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Clean and not overblown. I can send that as-is.
